transcript.text:
[Pohang Crabs with Ramyun Noodles]
[Can't Resist Snow Crabs!]
[Snow crab ramyun noodles at Pohang]
Fantastic~!
[All of that...?]
[Pohang is known for their Gwamegi (half-dried saury)]
[Wrap it up]
[Before] Can you finish all of that?!
[After]
[He was left speechless]
[Where is this place with clear waters?]
Hello everyone
I was invited here by the city of Pohang, so I'm here at Guryongpo (town)
This place is famous for their Gwamegi (half-dried saury) and they're in season right now
So I'll be promoting it today
By eating lots of it

I've never been here to eat Gwamegi
I really wonder what it will be like
[Hae Goong Fish Town] [Pohang, Nam-gu]
Hello~!
-Do you have a lot of Gwamegi? -Yes, lots of them
Eat it all
We have everything
[Try it]
[Cut~!]
[Rip~!]
[Whole-Gwamegi]
[Ready to be served]
[How good will it taste..?]
[Veggies first]
[Lots of it]
Wow, thank you

[Never-ending Gwamegi plates]
Do you have a warm soup?
We have snow crab ramyun noodles
-Please place the order later and we will bring it out -Thank you
We ripped these off by hand
These are just skinned
And these are cut with scissors
Gwamegi is our local specialty
Gwamegi Association is affiliated with our products to make sure they go through proper procedures
Guryongpo is known for their Gwamegi
I only had these in Seoul
I wonder how good they are here
They look really fresh Not dried up
Meaty and glossy
That's very moist
The ones I had before were dry and hard

These are soft and moist
Garlic too
It's not hard or tough to chew at all
Very soft and moist
It tastes even better wrapped in dried seaweed
Green onions, right?
Some of that
And garlic
Not fish either Delicious
Yummy~~!
I guess they rip it with hand too
Ripped into long pieces
Will it have a difference?
Just tasty?!
It kinda feels different?
Garlic on top of lettuce

Garlic goes well with it
That spice takes care of any fishiness
I bit myself
Dried seaweed
Dip the seaweed in chojang (chili paste with vinegar)
These should be good with seaweed too
Dip in sauce
Let's try it as a whole
Very big
This is half a fish, right?
They're bigger than I thought
Yum
Gwamegi stacks
[Garlic stacks too!]
Zucchini pancake
Chewy and tasty~

They're both chewy
Gwamegi is also enjoyed the most in winter
There are a lot of people that can't eat these because of the fishiness
But nowadays, they do extra research to better the quality of their Gwamegi to make it less fishy
I honestly think you should give these a try if you have been having trouble eating them
It's not fishy
Chewy
Can you see how fresh they are?
Even with some chojang
[They are filled with nutrients like protein, essential fatty acids, DHA, etc]
I thought Gwamegi was a name of a fish when I was younger
But these are Ggongchi (saury)
Thank you
They said it's good with kimchi
Wrap it
This really makes a good side dish for rice

You already had 2 plates of it?
Can you promote it for us?
[I'm too shy]
[Bringing in his wife]
Explain it in detail
Make an announcement for everyone
Everyone, Gwamegi is good for you
Good for your skin too
Please show love and support for Pohang, Guryongpo Gwamegi
I've been told that Gwamegi was not as successful last year because of high temperatures
This year, they were able to do it right
But they're still not as popular right now because of the pandemic
If you are a Gwamegi lover, you can get these delivered to your home, so please show a lot of support
If you eat too much of it, you might not feel good
So just eat a little bit
-And I'll make you snow crab ramyun noodles for you -Thank you

These are exceptional when you take them home and pan-fry them
Really? I better try it
[Snow crab ramyun noodles]
Thank you
This crab was caught just right here so it's fresh and meaty
-Enjoy -Thank you
Their ramyun looks amazing
Look at the soup
I must have a taste
That's so relieving
It's cooked so well
Crab too
Break it up like it
Pohang is also known for their crabs
The meat pulls right off when they're cooked
That's really sweet

It's sweet!
Amazing
Small piece of kimchi
This feels like a hangover meal
How about with some dried seaweed?
Tasty~
It's a first seeing this many crabs with ramyun
Right?
Snow crab legs like these
Domestic
2 of them
Let's pick the meat out
Pull it like this
Scrape it out
The meat tastes really sweet probably because it's locally caught fresh here in Pohang
Crab head bowl for the noodles

Why couldn't I think of this earlier?
Amazing
Guts too
Look at the meat
All that meat
Scrape it all out
The meat is sweet
You need to promote it too
Everyone!
This good food...
[Error...]
Come eat Pohang, Guryongpo's Gwamegi
Thank you
[Great job, sir!]
That was a great meal
Pohang is really famous for their Gwamegi

They're really fresh and tasty
Show support if you enjoy eating Gwamegi (link in the description)
I plan on taking the rest of them home to make other dishes like kimchi stew
Thanks everyone
[Where did she come this time?]
I'm here at Gwamegi Cultural Center
It's a place where you can taste and get to know more about Gwamegi
Hello~~
-I'm able to taste and purchase Gwamegi from here, right? -Yes
Delivery as well
There are lots of Gwamegi dishes
Pizza
Gwamegi Pizza?!?!
Gwamegi bread
Specialty bread too
This bread has collagen inside

[With sweet red bean]
Thank you
[Gwamegi Cultural Center]
Students drew these
[A wall of drawings by students]
This is the quality lab
They do hygiene management
And research to make it tastier
Saury has a lot of fish oil
So we test out the dry/aging stages both indoors and outdoors to test out the freshness
These are all fish oil
The process needs to be at a certain amount of time to get it to perfection
[Meeting with the mayor]
Our Gwamegi is dried and aged in such an ideal environment
That's why it's so precious and delicious
Our city provides a perfect environment for Gwamegi

That's why we built the cultural center here
For everyone to come see, experience, and share different cultures
And to enjoy what we have here
This place is really well-made
I was very impressed with the stuff here
It'd be nice to come check it out if you are in the area
Show lots of love and support~ [Subtitles by Hyun Choi] [antharoni@gmail.com]
